questions.question: Suppose we have a Nearest Centroid Classifier with a training set consisting of 10 samples from a normally distributed dataset with a mean of 5 and standard deviation of 2. What is the probability that the classifier will misclassify a new sample with a mean of 3? Assume that the classifier is correct on the training data.

A Nearest Centroid Classifier is used to classify iris flowers into two classes (Setosa and Versicolor) based on four features (sepal length, sepal width, petal length, petal width). If the classifier is trained on a dataset of 100 samples and has an accuracy of 90%, what is the average distance between the nearest centroid of each class?

A Nearest Centroid Classifier is used to classify handwritten digits into two classes (0-9) based on a feature vector of 10 pixels. If the training set consists of 500 samples and the classifier achieves 95% accuracy, and the number of nearest neighbors for each sample is 5, what is the average distance between the feature vectors of samples from the same class?
